title: SSE scan_from overlap with UTF-8 char-boundary fix is correct and must not be simplified away
description: The SSE stream parser maintains scan_from = old_len.saturating_sub(3) so that delimiter searches only re-scan the overlap region between old and new data (catching delimiters that span a chunk boundary). After setting scan_from, the code walks backward to a UTF-8 char boundary so that buf[scan_from..] never panics on a multibyte codepoint. This is correct: (1) the overlap of 3 bytes is the minimum needed to catch all four delimiter patterns (CRLF-CRLF = 4 bytes, LF-LF = 2 bytes, CR-CR = 2 bytes), and scanning from position 0 on every append would be O(total buffer) rather than O(new chunk); (2) the char-boundary walk is required because saturating_sub(3) can land inside a 4-byte UTF-8 character (e.g. an emoji at the chunk boundary). A future contributor might remove the char-boundary walk as 'unnecessary' if they do not realize that scan_from is used as a slice index into a String. The regression test sse_scan_from_char_boundary_fix documents the exact panic scenario using 'a\u{1F600}' (1+4 bytes) and verifies both the bug trigger and the fix.
